questions.question_text: You are in a hurry and are driving at 70 km/h. What do you do?
You are driving at about 40 km/h, the same speed as the oncoming traffic. What do you do?
You are driving at 30 to 40 km/h and must continue straight ahead. What do you do here?
You are driving at about 50 km/h and suddenly see an oncoming car. What do you do?
You are driving at about 80 km/h. What do you do?
When may you use the emergency telephone on a motorway?
You have reduced speed to about 30 km/h because you want to turn right at the intersection. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are driving at about 40 km/h. What do you do?
You are driving at about 80 km/h. What do you do?
You are driving on the right-hand side of a one-way road. What should you pay particular attention to a little further ahead?
You are driving at about 40 km/h and need to pass the stationary vehicle. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You must continue straight ahead on the road with a bend. The two cars are stationary at the roadside. What do you do?
You are driving at about 50 km/h. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You have caught up with the two slowly moving cars with caravans. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You must continue straight ahead at the intersection. What do you do?
You want to turn right into the side road. The lane to the right is clear. What do you do?
You want to turn left here. There is no traffic coming from the right. What do you do?
You are driving at about 30 to 40 km/h and must go straight ahead at the intersection. What do you do?
You want to turn right at this intersection. The lane to the left is clear and there are no cyclists behind you. What do you do?
You need to continue straight ahead at the intersection. There are no cyclists behind you. What do you do?
What is the maximum permitted length of a vehicle including its load?
You have just got out of the car here. Which stopping and parking rules apply?

answers.1: I may park the car here for one hour on Saturday morning at 7 a.m.
answers.2: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes on Saturday morning at 7 a.m.

questions.question_text: The car ahead is moving so slowly that the lorry overtakes it. What do you do?
What does the police officer's hand signal mean?
You have just got out of the car. Which stopping and parking rule applies here?

answers.2: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.
answers.3: Stopping and parking are prohibited here.
